danger_signals.meaning: Primary antagonist faction; mechanical infiltrator threat
Faction-aligned hostile units; distinguish from human threats

safety_signals.meaning: Player-controlled law enforcement entity; protagonist faction

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Urban Detroit setting; police/law enforcement infrastructure

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mechanical cyborgs; distinct from human enemies

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Presence of Terminator units and cyborg enemies in urban environments
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy patrol patterns; aggressive cyborg behavior distinct from human enemy types

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: RoboCop protagonist; law enforcement-themed level design

environmental_storytelling.example: RoboCop and Terminator franchises merged in single game world; visual/thematic clash between 1987 cyberpunk law enforcement dystopia and 1984 post-apocalyptic AI threat
Human criminals vs. mechanical Terminator units signal different threat origins and factions operating in the game world

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Law enforcement protagonist; responds to threats; primary agent in conflict against Terminator threat
Cyborg enemies; mechanically distinct from human foes; represent Skynet faction presence

notes: Source material provides metadata and developer information but lacks detailed visual/behavioral analysis of world-state communication systems. Game represents IP crossover combining RoboCop's OmniCorp dystopia with Terminator's Skynet threat narrative. Regional differences documented (US vs European staff image cropping) suggest localization occurred but content details not provided in source.
